def: s.m. See s.v. shaṭ. adj. Six
subs: s.m. The six parts of the body (viz. the arms, the legs, the head, and the waist); — the six auxiliary or supplementary branches (aṅg) of sacred science (i.e. Grammar; Prosody; Pronunciation; the meanings of unusual terms; Astronomy; and the ritual of the Hindū religion); — a set of six particular articles of offering at the first oblation of rice, &c. to the manes of deceased ancestors
and vulg. shaṭ-ānan, adj. & s.m. Six-faced; — an epithet of Kārttikeya, the son of Śiva and god of war
adj. & s.m. Six-footed; — a bee; a louse
s.m. The six seasons (see r̤itu); — shaḍ-ras, vulg. shaṭ-ras, s.m. The six flavours or tastes (see ras)
vulg. shaṭ-darśan, and shaṇ-darśan, s.m. The six systems of philosophy ( = shaṭ-śāstr, q.v.); — a man who is versed in the six systems of philosophy
s.m. The six philosophical systems of the Hindūs (viz. the Nyāya; the Vaiśeshika; the Mīmāṅsā; the Vedānta; the Sāṅkhya; and the Pāṭanjala)

s.m. pl. The six acts or duties enjoined on a Brāhmaṇ (viz. teaching the Vedas, holy study, offering sacrifices, conducting them for others, giving and accepting gifts); — the six acts allowable to a Brāhmaṇ for subsistence (viz. gleaning, accepting gifts, asking alms, agriculture, trade, tending cattle, or, according to some, lending money at interest); — six acts that may be performed by means of magical texts as taught in the Tantras (viz. killing, infatuating, enthralling, expelling, exciting animosity, and the stopping or privation of any faculty); — a Brāhmaṇ skilled in the above six acts; an adept in the Tantra magical rites
or shaṭ-konā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Hexangular; — a hexagon, any six-angled figure; the thunderbolt of Indra
adj. & s.m. Six-fold, six times; — an assemblage of six qualities or properties
vulg. shaṭ-vadan, adj. & s.m. = shaḍ-ānan, q.v.
adj. Of six kinds or descriptions; six-fold, six times; in six ways

def: adj. corr. of shoḍaśa, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of شدّ 'to make hard, firm, or strong,' &c.
def: s.m. Rendering hard or strong, &c.; intensifying; emphasizing; pitching of the voice (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: s.m. Emphasizing (in reading, &c.); intensity; emphasis, stress; force; severity.

def: s.m. = shaddā, q.v. s.m. The banners that are carried with the taʻziya in the Moḥarram.

ety: prob. corr. of A. شدة, fr. shadd, q.v.
def: s.m. The banners that are carried with the taʻziya in the Moḥarram.
s.m. See s.v. shadd. s.m. Rendering hard or strong, &c.; intensifying; emphasizing; pitching of the voice (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: s.m. Emphasizing (in reading, &c.); intensity; emphasis, stress; force; severity.

ety: Pārsī sāṭ; old P. shiyāti; Pehl. shātish; Zend shāiti, fr. shā; see shād
def: s.f. Pleasure, delight, joy, rejoicing, festivity; marriage; a wedding; a marriage-feast
subs: To rejoice; to make a feast, to feast; to perform a religious ceremony previous to entering a new house; — to marry
(-kī), To get (a boy, or girl) married (to, -se)
(-kī), To marry (a child, to); — to dispose (of, — a horse, &c.)
s.f. Death from joy; dying easily, an easy death
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شادی (šâdi). Equivalent to شاد (śād) + ـی (-ī).

ety: v.r. of شدّ 'to be hard,' &c.
def: adj. Violent, strong, vehement, intense, severe, rigorous; difficult, arduous; distressing, afflicting, grievous; heavy; grave; heinous.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَدِید (šadīd), from Arabic شَدِيد (šadīd).

ety: shādī+āna = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & s.m. Relating to marriage or to rejoicings; — music and singing at marriages or on other festive occasions; a band; marriage fees or presents (made by a cultivator to the landlord, &c.)
subs: To play festive music, to rejoice; to triumph.

def: s.f. pl. (of shādī, after the model of the Ar.), 'Rejoicings'; fees paid at marriages.

ety: shād, q.v.+mān, fr. Zend aff. man = S. मन्
def: adj. Pleased, &c. ( = shād, q.v.). adj. Pleased, delighted, exulting, joyful, happy, glad, cheerful, rejoicing (in); (in comp.) full (of); much
subs: adj. Full of water or moisture, moist, humid, succulent; fresh, verdant; pleasant, agreeable
adj. Fortunate, well off; voluptuous; — s.f. A dancing-girl, an adulteress, a strumpet
adj. Rejoicing, exulting; joyous, happy, contented

def: s.f. Rejoicing, joy, delight, pleasure, gladness.

ety: act. part. of شيع 'to be published or divulged,' &c.
def: adj. Spreading, becoming spread, published, divulged, revealed; notorious; apparent, manifest; — distressed
subs: v.n. To be spread abroad, be published, be propagated, &c.

def: s.m. A bougie, pessary, suppository; an enema.

ety: for A. شفاعة, inf. n. of شفع 'to intercede,' &c.
def: s.f. Intercession, recommendation, entreaty, deprecation

ety: inf. n. of شفق 'to fear'; 'to be compassionate,' &c.
def: s.m. (f.?) Fear; — affection, kindness, &c. ( = shafaqat, q.v.); — the redness of the sky between sunset and nightfall, evening twilight
subs: s.f. The northern lights, the aurora borealis.

ety: for A. شفقة; see shafaq
def: s.f. Affection, kindness, benignity, tenderness, pity, compassion, mercy, clemency; condolence; favourable inclination, favour
subs: (-par), To show kindness (to), to take compassion (on), to commiserate
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَفَقَت (šafaqat), borrowed from Arabic شَفَقَة (šafaqa), from شَفَقَ (šafaqa). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi شفقت (śfqt).

ety: fr. shafaq
def: adj. Of or relating to the evening twilight; like or resembling the evening twilight.

ety: for A. شفة; orig. شفہة, or شفوة
def: s.m. The lip; the mouth.
s.m. A red peach; a nectarine.

ety: act. part. of شفع 'to join oneself to, to aid; to make petition or intercession,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Deprecating; interceding; — an intercessor, mediator, advocate, patron.

ety: rel. n. fr. shāfěʻ
def: s.m. Name of the Imām Abū-ʻabduʼl-lah Moḥammad-bin-Idrīs, one of the chiefs of the four principal sects of the Moḥammadan religion; — a follower of the Imām Shāfeʻī.

ety: intens. n. fr. شفّ 'to be thin or fine,' &c.
def: adj. Very thin; translucent; transparent; clear, pellucid.
s.f. (fr. shaffāf), Transparency, clearness; perspicuity.

ety: v.n. fr. شفع; see shafāʻat, and shufʻa
def: s.m. Intercessor; advocate, patron; — a possessor of the right of pre-emption, one who demands and obtains, as a neighbour (or a partner), the right of purchasing a house, or a piece of land, that is to be sold
subs: s.m. The occupant of ground which is simply in the neighbourhood of another estate
s.m. The occupant of ground in actual contact, or intermixed with, another estate (the right of pre-emption is stronger in the latter than in the former case).

ety: for A. شافٍ shafiṉ, act. part. of شفو to restore to convalescence'
def: adj. & s.m. Restoring to convalescence, health-giving, healing, sanative, salutary; — relieving from doubt (an answer); categorical, decisive, positive, final; — a restorer to health, a healer
subs: 'The true or actual healer'; and shāfī-ě-mut̤laq, 'The absolute healer'; epithets of the Deity.

def: s.m. A kind of Persian character or handwriting.

ety: v.n. fr. شفق, see shafaqat
def: adj. & s.m. Affectionate, kind, tender, merciful, compassionate; — a kind and benevolent adviser, a kind friend (syn. mushfiq, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شَفِيق (šafīq).

def: s.m. lit. 'The rough plum'; a peach; — the bloom of the peach; the colour of the flower of the peach tree.

ety: inf. n. of شغب 'to excite,' &c.
def: s.m. Noise, clamour, confusion of cries or shouts, tumult, uproar.

ety: = S. शृगाल
def: s.m. A jackal
subs: s.m. lit. 'Jackal-eater'; a term applied to a gang of free-booters on the Oude frontier.

ety: = shagāl, q.v.
def: s.m. A jackal.

ety: act. part. of شغل 'to be or become busied;' &c.
def: adj. Busying, occupying; busy, occupied, employed; attentive; intent.

ety: cf. ćākar
def: s.m. A scholar, student, apprentice; pupil, disciple; a servant, boy, groom
subs: s.m. A complete scholar; an apt scholar; a favourite pupil
(-ko), To bind or adopt as an apprentice, &c.
(-kā), To become the pupil (of); to become an apprentice (to).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَاگِرْد (šāgird), from Middle Persian [script needed] (hšʾklt' /⁠hašāgird⁠/, “disciple, pupil”), from Proto-Iranian *hašā-kr̥táh-. Compare Bengali শাগরেদ (śagred). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi شاگِرْد (śagrd).

def: s.f. Pupilage; apprenticeship; learning, study
subs: (ke pās?), To serve (one's) apprenticeship (with); to learn or study (with).

ety: inf. n. of شغل 'to occupy, busy, employ,' &c.
def: s.m. Business, occupation, employment, labour, study; anything to occupy or divert; diversion, pastime, amusement
subs: To pursue a business or occupation, to employ oneself; to be busy, be occupied, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شغل (šuġl), from Arabic شُغْل (šuḡl).

ety: Zend khshaya, rt. khshi = S. क्षि(क्षयति)

subs: s.m. A kind of plum; a prune, Prunus cerabus
adj. Royal, noble, generous; — s.m. A royal falcon

s.m. The boy (of the same age, height, and figure as the bridegroom, and similarly dressed and decorated) who accompanies the bridegroom to the bride's house on the night of the barāt; — the groom or attendant who rides behind a great man (also shah-bālā)
s.m. 'Royal oak'; the oak; the chesnut
s.m. Chief port or harbour; (local) custom-house; — a harbour-master; the receiver-general of duties or tribute
(and shah-par), s.m. The largest or strongest feather (in a bird's wing)
(and shah-tīr), s.m. The large beam (of a house); a beam
s.m. 'The monarch of the East,' the sun
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat used on great occasions
s.m. A village on a river (in front of a king's palace); — the highway
s.m. 'Old man of the sea'; one of the seven demons held in awe by Moḥammadan women
s.f. 'King's road,' the highway, main road; a principal street
(and shah-zādagī), s.f. The state, or the youth, of a prince; nobility (syn. amīrī); the minority of a prince; — youthful folly; foolish pride
(and shah-zāda), s.m. 'King's son,' the prince-royal; a prince; (in the language of women) a dear son
s.f. A princess; a queen; — the state of prince or princess
(and shah-sawār), s.m. A good rider, an expert horseman
adj. Pleasant-paced, having easy paces (a horse); — s.m. The royal pace, name of a particular pace of a horse
s.m. 'King of the firmament,' the sun
'King of heroes,' a title of the Khalīfa ʻAlī
s.m. 'Monarch of the East,' the sun
s.f. A celebrated epic poem, containing a history of the kings of Persia (written by Firdausī about the year of Christ 1000)

(and shah-nishīn), s.m. The king's seat (i.e. a gallery or balcony projecting from the palace, where the king shows himself to his people); a balcony, gallery; the principal seat in an assembly, a president's seat
(and shah-wār), adj. Royal, princely, noble; like a king; fit for a king, befitting a king; — s.m. A fine royal pearl.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شاه (šāh), from Middle Persian 𐭬𐭫𐭪𐭠 (mlkʾ /⁠šāh⁠/) from Old Persian 𐏋 (XŠ /⁠xšāyaθiya⁠/, “king”). Compare Punjabi ਸ਼ਾਹ (śāh) / شاہ (śāh), Gujarati શાહ (śāh), Marathi शहा (śahā), Bengali শাহ (śah).

ety: contrac. of shāh
def: s.m. & adj. A king, &c. (see shāh)
subs: s.f. A term in chess (when the king only can move)
(-ko), To praise falsely, to flatter; — to give a paper-kite string
s.m. (in Chess), Check to the king by a castle or rook; a divergent check on the part of the knight (or any inferior piece), at once attacking the king and the castle
s.f. The great artery, the aorta; the jugular vein (in the arm, &c.)
adj. Very strong or powerful, stout, gallant, brave, heroic
s.f. Great strength, might; valour, heroism, prowess
s.f.(?), Check-mate. (For compounds not found in this place see s.v. shāh, for which shah may in most cases be substituted.)

ety: prob. shah+āb
def: s.m. A red colour (said to be the colour extracted from bastard saffron).

ety: prob. for A. ashhād, pl. of shāhid
def: s.m. Evidences, witnesses.

ety: for A. شہادة, inf. n. of شہد 'to give evidence,' &c.
def: s.f. Evidence, testimony, witness; martyrdom
subs: v.n. To give evidence, to bear witness
s.m. 'The day of martyrdom (of Husain),' the tenth of Moḥarram.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَهَادَت (šahādat), borrowed from Arabic شَهَادَة (šahāda), from شَهِدَ (šahida).

ety: for A. شہامة, inf. n. of شہم 'to be hardy, or strong,' &c.
def: s.f. Stoutness (of heart), bravery; generosity, nobleness of mind; ingenuity, sharpness, quickness of parts, penetration; agility, swiftness.

ety: shāh q.v.+āna = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. Of or belonging to a king, royal, princely; splendid, magnificent; — s.m. A dress worn by a bridegroom, a wedding-dress.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شاهانه (šāhāna).

def: adj. (f. -ī) = shāhāna, q.v. adj. Of or belonging to a king, royal, princely; splendid, magnificent; — s.m. A dress worn by a bridegroom, a wedding-dress.

def: s.m. King of kings, lord of lords; an emperor; a title adopted by the old kings of Persia
s.m. = shāhanshāh, q.v. s.m. King of kings, lord of lords; an emperor; a title adopted by the old kings of Persia

adj. Imperial, royal, princely; s.f. Empire, imperial dignity, monarchy.
s.f. = shāhanshāhī, q.v. adj. Imperial, royal, princely; s.f. Empire, imperial dignity, monarchy.
s.m. Honey (syn. madhu); anything very sweet
subs: lit. 'A sweet honeyed knife'; a foe in the garb of a friend
v.t. (colloq.), 'To apply honey and lick'; to keep a useless thing very carefully
v.n. (colloq.), To sow dissension (between), to set by the ears.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَهْد (šahd), borrowed from Arabic شَهْد (šahd).

def: adj. Imperial, royal, regal, kingly; — s.m. A follower of the sect of 'Alī; — s.f. Imperial dignity, dominion, sovereignty, reign, royalty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَاهِی (šāhī).

def: adj. & s.f. = shāhī, q.v. adj. Imperial, royal, regal, kingly; — s.m. A follower of the sect of 'Alī; — s.f. Imperial dignity, dominion, sovereignty, reign, royalty.

ety: act. part. of شہد 'to bear witness,' &c.
def: s.m. One who bears witness, a witness, deponent; (in P. shāhid or shāhad, m. & f.) a sweetheart, a beloved object; a handsome man; a beautiful woman; a female friend
subs: s.m. A witness of facts, an eye-witness
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شاهد (šāhid), from Arabic شَاهِد (šāhid). Compare Punjabi شاہد / ਸ਼ਾਹਦ (śāhad).

ety: v.n. fr. شہد 'to give testimony,' &c.
def: s.m. A witness; one who is slain in the cause of (the Mohammadan) religion (on the field of battle, fighting against unbelievers, &c.); a martyr
subs: v.n. To be slain in battle against unbelievers; to become a martyr, to suffer martyrdom; to come by one's death unjustly, or by accident, &c.; — to fall desperately in love (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَهِید (šahīd), borrowed from Arabic شَهِيد (šahīd), from شَهِدَ (šahida).

ety: shāhid, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Witness, testimony, evidence; attestation, deposition (syn. iz̤hār).

ety: act. part. of شہق 'to rise high'
def: adj. High, lofty (a mountain, or a building, &c.).

ety: Zend śaêna; S. श्येन; — A. shāhīn, fr. the P.
def: s.m. A white falcon, a royal falcon; the gerfalcon (cf. sīmurg̠).
wety: From Classical Persian شاهین (šāhīn).

def: s.m. contrac. of shāhīn, q.v.

adj. = mashhūr, q.v. part. adj. Well-known, celebrated, notable, noted, famous, notorious; apparent, conspicuous, manifest, public, published, given out, proclaimed, divulged, reported. — mashhūr karnā or kar-denā, v.t. To give out, publish, proclaim
subs: To be or become famous or notorious as; — to be given out, reported, made public
It is well known; — it is given out, or reported, &c.

ety: fem. of ashhal, rt. شہل
def: adj. Having a tinge of blue or gray, or of red, or green, in the black (of the eye); having dark-gray eyes with a shade of red.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شَهْلَاء (šahlāʔ), feminine singular of أَشْهَل (ʔašhal).

subs: or shahnāʼī-ćī, s.m. A player on the shahnā, a piper, &c.

Cognate with Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “dominion, kingdom”), and Parthian 𐭇𐭔𐭕𐭓 (ḥštr /⁠xšahr⁠/). Doublet of کھاتا (khātā).

Cognate with Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “dominion, kingdom”), and Parthian 𐭇𐭔𐭕𐭓 (ḥštr /⁠xšahr⁠/). Doublet of کھاتا (khātā).

Those branches, to whom the tree no longer seems good.

def: adj. Six
subs: s.m. The six parts of the body (viz. the arms, the legs, the head, and the waist); — the six auxiliary or supplementary branches (aṅg) of sacred science (i.e. Grammar; Prosody; Pronunciation; the meanings of unusual terms; Astronomy; and the ritual of the Hindū religion); — a set of six particular articles of offering at the first oblation of rice, &c. to the manes of deceased ancestors
and vulg. shaṭ-ānan, adj. & s.m. Six-faced; — an epithet of Kārttikeya, the son of Śiva and god of war
adj. & s.m. Six-footed; — a bee; a louse
s.m. The six seasons (see r̤itu); — shaḍ-ras, vulg. shaṭ-ras, s.m. The six flavours or tastes (see ras)
vulg. shaṭ-darśan, and shaṇ-darśan, s.m. The six systems of philosophy ( = shaṭ-śāstr, q.v.); — a man who is versed in the six systems of philosophy
s.m. The six philosophical systems of the Hindūs (viz. the Nyāya; the Vaiśeshika; the Mīmāṅsā; the Vedānta; the Sāṅkhya; and the Pāṭanjala)

s.m. pl. The six acts or duties enjoined on a Brāhmaṇ (viz. teaching the Vedas, holy study, offering sacrifices, conducting them for others, giving and accepting gifts); — the six acts allowable to a Brāhmaṇ for subsistence (viz. gleaning, accepting gifts, asking alms, agriculture, trade, tending cattle, or, according to some, lending money at interest); — six acts that may be performed by means of magical texts as taught in the Tantras (viz. killing, infatuating, enthralling, expelling, exciting animosity, and the stopping or privation of any faculty); — a Brāhmaṇ skilled in the above six acts; an adept in the Tantra magical rites
or shaṭ-konā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Hexangular; — a hexagon, any six-angled figure; the thunderbolt of Indra
adj. & s.m. Six-fold, six times; — an assemblage of six qualities or properties

adj. Of six kinds or descriptions; six-fold, six times; in six ways
